tax: Placed at family rank, White-Water Fluvials is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an aerated white-water body bound to a cascade. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a white-water body with its own fall.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a churning foam-body at a fall. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a scoured plunge-pool and high spray-line — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: White-Water Fluvials occupies cataracts and cascades. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure animates a cascade's churn and recur with the fall keeps running, sustaining themselves on kinetic energy of falling water.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: churns at its fall, drags prey in to batter. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: aerated foam-body and motion-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: battering, drowning, and spray. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, White-Water Fluvials matters because makes fords near falls lethal. It lets survey reports sort white-water hazards from still-water ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: White-Water Fluvials is the family-rank grouping of aerated moving-water elementals of falls and cascades within the True Elementals. Its members share a foaming aerated body bound to falling water, and divide below into genus groups by fall height and flow rate. Born of moving water, anchored to its fall.
